Model: Opus 4.8 . Effort: high . (This turn is synthesis, not a Fable burn. I am dogfooding the recommendation line we are about to wire in.)  **TL;DR:** Your two sweeps handed us near-perfect Fable fuel, because the best burns are ones with a *knowable* answer you can score. The flagship burn is a two-for-one: the cross-product coherence audit run as Fable-1M vs Opus-chunked. It ships a real deliverable (the missing portfolio health metric plus dark-zone reconciliation) AND empirically tests the single premium that justifies Fable's price, the 1M whole-corpus claim.  **Status:** Real backlog binned into the rubric below. Scored burn plan ready. Nothing written yet; the durable rubric and comparison ledger get created as the byproduct of the first Track B run, not as separate machinery.  **Your move:** pick the burns to launch (I recommend the flagship plus A1). Say go and I start the flagship this turn.  ---  ## Binned: your live backlog against the rubric  Signals: LH long-horizon . WC whole-corpus-1M . HS hard-synthesis . AF adversarial . CE high-cost-error-weak-catchnet.  ### Track A: Fable executes real work  | Item (source) | Signals fired | Fit | Effort | |---|---|---|---| | **A1. Design the upstream control for the intent governance-gap cluster** (scout #5: SIG-F-001 closure-policy-gap, 93d, has resisted multiple fixes) | HS, CE, meta-layer | HIGH | M | | **A2. Build decision surfaces for the two 48-day positioning items** (overwatch: Parallax three-axis, 3 intent positioning DDRs) | HS, AF | HIGH | M | | **A3. Whole-portfolio coherence audit + design the missing numeric health metric** (scout #4 measurement void, plus 7 dark zones) | WC, HS, CE | HIGH | M | | A4. Crucible first native synthesis (your project set, not the sweeps) | HS, WC | HIGH | M |  ### Track B: comparison harnesses (each produces durable harness knowledge)  | Item | What it tests | Ground truth to score against | Effort | |---|---|---|---| | **B4 (FLAGSHIP). A3 run twice: Fable-1M vs Opus-4.8-chunked** | The 1M whole-corpus premium itself | Coherence gaps either surface or they do not; you compare the two audits side by side | M | | **B2. cast mtime-integrity root cause: Fable vs Opus vs Sonnet** (scout #3, a real pending) | Hard diagnosis accuracy | Known: 315 attributed to legit batch writers. You can score which model separates drift from writer-noise correctly | M | | **B1. Disconfirmation on the cast conscience-board tension: Fable vs Opus** (scout open tension) | Adversarial sharpness | Known: the blocker is stale (weekly cadence ran through it). Does Fable catch the supersession reasoning faster | S | | **B3. cast residue characterization: Sonnet vs Fable** (overwatch, 332 files) | The mechanical floor, a proof-negative | Idempotency dry-run is deterministic. Proves Sonnet suffices so you stop over-paying here | S |  ### Route elsewhere (this is the classifier working, not idle items)  - Adjudicate the two matured falsification gates (scout #1): Opus 4.8, medium. Cheap decisive reads, no Fable edge. - cast residue disposal (overwatch): Sonnet 5, mechanical. - conscience-board supersession marker: trivial, any tier. - Core-root allowlist call, positioning L2 calls, Layer-4.2 (window closes today): yours to make, not a model's.  ## Two burn-mechanics flags  1. **Do not fan out Fable subagents.** Your reference_subagent_1m_context_gate memory says Fable fleets can hard-fail "Usage credits required," which would tip you into extra spend, the one thing you are trying to avoid. Run Fable burns as main-thread or sequential single runs at high or max effort. Effort spend is in-plan, so max effort is free headroom to use. 2. **Sequence for evidence.** Run B4 first: it is the highest-information burn and it doubles as delivering A3. Then A1. If Fable closes the 93-day governance loop that multiple Opus passes could not, that is your single strongest data point for what to pay for.  ## The wiring, now concrete  1. **Convention (live as of this message):** substantive turns open with `Model: X . Effort: Y` and, when the rubric fires, `FABLE-SUITED, fallback Opus 4.8, reason: Z`. I used it at the top of this turn. 2. **Durable rubric + comparison ledger:** these get written as the byproduct of the first Track B run, so the ledger schema matches real columns (model, task, effort, wall-time, scored-delta, verdict). The rubric lands as a memory plus a model-tier dimension appended to  /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/frameworks/methodology-library/meta/signal-scoring.md  3. **Hook (mechanism, needs your nod):** a Stop hook that nags when a substantive plan ships without a model or effort recommendation, parallel to your autonomy-grant and closure-discipline hooks. I am holding this one for your explicit go because it changes every session, unlike the reversible file writes.  Say go and I launch B4 (Fable-1M coherence audit) this turn, which starts the real burn and populates the ledger's first two rows.
